response_text:
**MEMORANDUM**

**TO:** All Employees  
**FROM:** Sarah Chen, CEO  
**DATE:** October 24, 2024  
**SUBJECT:** Launch of Our Flexible Hybrid Work Policy – Effective November 1

We are implementing a new Flexible Hybrid work model designed to balance the collaborative energy of in-person work with the focus and flexibility of remote work. After six months of pilot data, employee feedback, and infrastructure upgrades, we are ready to make this transition permanent.

**THE POLICY**

Beginning November 1, all employees will operate on a **"3-2 Flexible"** schedule:

*   **Three days remote:** Choose your most productive work-from-home days. We trust you to align these with your team’s needs and project deadlines.
*   **Two days in-office:** Each team will designate two "Anchor Days" (Tuesday/Wednesday for most teams) when everyone is expected on-site for collaboration, sprint planning, and face-to-face problem-solving.
*   **Core collaboration hours:** Whether remote or in-office, be available for synchronous work between 10:00 AM and 3:00 PM in your local time zone. Outside these hours, you control your deep-work schedule.
*   **Equipment & security:** We will provide ergonomic home office setups (monitor, keyboard, chair) and require VPN usage on all networks. Full guidelines are attached.

**WHY WE’RE MAKING THIS CHANGE**

Our pilot data showed a 22% increase in feature delivery velocity on remote days and a 40% increase in cross-functional idea generation on in-office anchor days. More importantly, 89% of you reported improved work-life balance under a flexible structure.

This policy also positions us to hire exceptional talent regardless of geography while preserving the culture and mentorship that happens when we share physical space. We are not becoming a "remote-first" company; we are becoming a "flexibility-first" company that uses the right environment for the right work.

**WHAT YOU NEED TO DO NEXT**

**By November 1:**
1.  **Schedule a 1:1 with your manager** to confirm your team’s Anchor Days and discuss any scheduling constraints.
2.  **Submit equipment requests** through the new Work-From-Home Portal (IT will ship approved items by November 8).
3.  **Update your calendar** to reflect your recurring in-office days so colleagues can plan accordingly.
4.  **Complete the 15-minute Remote Security Training** in LearningHub (mandatory by November 5).

**By November 15:**
*   Test your home setup during a remote day and report any technical gaps to IT.
*   Participate in your team’s "Collaboration Charter" workshop to establish norms for hybrid meetings and async communication.

The Operations team has converted 30% of our office space to hot-desking and added six new Zoom-enabled conference rooms. If you have questions, join the #hybrid-work-policy Slack channel or attend the Q&A session this Friday at 2:00 PM in the main conference room (streamed live).

This is an evolution, not a revolution. We will review the policy quarterly based on your feedback and project metrics.

Thank you for building something great—whether that happens at your kitchen table or at Desk 42.

Sarah

**Attachments:** Remote Security Checklist, Equipment Request Form, Team Anchor Day Schedule
